signals.headline: Trump Implements Naval Blockade of Iran | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Trump Implements Naval Blockade of Iran | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**

President Trump has begun a US naval blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz to increase pressure on Tehran[2][3]. Trump stated that Iran has called wanting to "work a deal," indicating both sides remain open to negotiations[3].

signals.raw:
**Oil Market Impact | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence** — Oil prices have declined on ceasefire negotiation hopes, and the S&P 500 has recovered Iran war losses[2]. A sustained blockade would support elevated oil prices; a ceasefire extension would likely pressure them.

No major breakthrough or resolution has been *confirmed* — negotiations remain in preliminary stages with the ball described as bein

signals.headline: Ripple's RLUSD Stablecoin Fully Rolls Out on XRP Ledger and Ethereum | YES | MED**
signals.raw:
Ripple's RLUSD Stablecoin Fully Rolls Out on XRP Ledger and Ethereum | YES | MED**  
Official full-scale rollout confirmed as of April 13, driving record XRPL transaction volume and capital influx, with SEC lawsuit fully resolved and XRP ETF approvals in final stages.[4]

signals.headline: US and Iran Eyeing Fresh Talks Before Ceasefire Expires | ONGOING | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US and Iran Eyeing Fresh Talks Before Ceasefire Expires | ONGOING | HIGH confidence**

The US and Iran are discussing another round of negotiations to extend or replace the two-week ceasefire announced April 7, which expires around April 21[1][2]. Recent talks in Islamabad over the weekend ended without a deal[1].

signals.headline: XRP ETF Inflows Hit $119
signals.raw:
XRP ETF Inflows Hit $119.6M for Week Ending April 11 | YES | HIGH**  
Seven spot XRP ETFs now approach $1B in assets under management, with Switzerland directing 70% of global flows ($157M) into XRP products—the strongest weekly inflow since December.[1]  
SEC and CFTC classified XRP as a digital commodity in March, boosting institutional momentum amid market fear.[1]

signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Rate at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Holds Rate at 2.5% Amid Rising Inflation Pressures | NO rate change | HIGH confidence**

The **Bank of Korea kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 2.5 percent on April 10, 2026**, marking the 7th consecutive month without adjustment.[4] This was a unanimous decision by the Monetary Policy Board and widely anticipated by markets.

signals.headline: **No rate cuts expected in 2026**: Analysts expect no changes to the policy rate for the remainder of the year as inflat
signals.raw: **No rate cuts expected in 2026**: Analysts expect no changes to the policy rate for the remainder of the year as inflation pressures and currency weakness narrow room for monetary accommodation.[5]

signals.headline: **Growth concerns**: The BOK expects 2026 economic growth to fall below 2 percent due to the weakened Korean won, which
signals.raw: **Growth concerns**: The BOK expects 2026 economic growth to fall below 2 percent due to the weakened Korean won, which recently rose to around 1,520 won per U.S. dollar.[4]

signals.headline: **Stagflation risk acknowledged**: International institutions have warned of potential stagflation (simultaneous growth
signals.raw:
**Stagflation risk acknowledged**: International institutions have warned of potential stagflation (simultaneous growth slowdown and price increases), though the Bank of Korea currently views this as unlikely under baseline conditions.[7]

The decision reflects the central bank's prioritization of currency stability and inflation management over growth support.[3]

signals.raw: **Ceasefire Extension Prospects | UNCERTAIN | MED confidence** — While both sides are discussing renewed talks, there is no confirmed agreement yet. The compressed timeline (talks needed before April 21 expiration) creates significant uncertainty[1][2].

signals.headline: CLARITY Act Heads to Senate Banking Committee Markup Vote in Late April | NO | LOW**
signals.raw:
CLARITY Act Heads to Senate Banking Committee Markup Vote in Late April | NO | LOW**  
Endorsed by Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent; could enable banks to use XRP for liquidity, but no confirmation of passage or vote outcome yet—Senate returns April 13 with midterm delays looming.[1][3][5]
